config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang saya anggap penting, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun saat hak dasar orang tetap dilindungi, terutama untuk kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil. Kalau kepentingan umum selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah disalahgunakan untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should automatically take priority over individual rights. From a U.S. perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it can become an excuse for the majority or the government to trample minorities, dissenters, or unpopular people in the name of the "greater good." I do think there are real cases where public safety or public health justify limits on individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, lawful, and strongly justified. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first and then balances them carefully against genuine collective needs.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang dekat dengan konteks Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau tindakan sewenang-wenang terhadap orang kecil dan kelompok minoritas. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik yang bisa membatasi kebebasan individu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang melindungi martabat tiap orang sambil mencari keseimbangan yang adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that principle is too easy to abuse, because governments and majorities often claim to act for the common good while actually silencing minorities, critics, or inconvenient people. I do think there are limited cases, like emergencies or public health crises, where some rights can be temporarily restricted, but only with clear rules, strong justification, and real oversight. In my view, individual rights are not an obstacle to a healthy society; they are one of the main reasons a healthy society exists in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak individu itu yang melindungi warga dari penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh negara atau mayoritas. Saya setuju ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana yang bisa menuntut pembatasan sementara, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan diawasi, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan umum tanpa menginjak martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In the United States, we have a long history of seeing how “the greater good” can be used to justify discrimination, censorship, and government overreach, so I am very wary of any broad claim that collective interests come first. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and emergencies can justify narrow limits if they are clearly necessary, temporary, and accountable. To me, a free society works best when individual rights are the default and collective needs are handled with strict restraint, not as a blank check to override people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
